model_families: RSF, XGB-Cox, CoxNet, LightGBM, CatBoost, LogReg
ensemble_method: rank-average across 5×3=15 (feature_set, model) cells per endpoint
notes: Phase 1 grid; CV ~0.844. Death rank-avg likely miscalibrated relative to small public LB cohort.
Audit probe — confirms strict_time_aligned does NOT transfer (CV ~0.94, LB 0.692). Tier-5 forbidden.
Tier-1 purity left ~0.05 LB hep on the table; trajectory features we audited away are organizer-blessed signal.

feature_sets_used: landmark_3y (49 features = baseline_v1 + LOCF + slope-since-baseline at 3y horizon)
notes: First clean Tier-2 submission. Hep CV 0.880 ± 0.065; established the landmark recipe.

short_description: 50/50 rank blend of landmark_3y_RSF + permissive ensemble
notes: Cross-method diversity (landmark × permissive, OOF Spearman 0.478) yielded +0.069 LB over phase2_landmark_3y.
Within 0.001 of single-3y (LB 0.811). Within-method ensembling exhausted — diversity must come from method class.

short_description: 3-way blend: landmark + permissive + tuned XGB-Cox baseline
notes: Down 0.051 from 2-way. The honest XGB-Cox/baseline_v1 third pulled distribution toward Tier-1 which under-rewards on LB.
+0.016 LB over 50/50. Permissive component carries 70% — it is the engine of the blend, not landmark.

short_description: Replaces 3-member permissive with single rsf_longitudinal_summary; OOF re-stacked w=0.23/0.77
notes: CV-LB inversion #1: +0.014 CV, −0.010 LB. Removing the 3-XGB redundancy hurt LB despite CV gain.

short_description: Cross-endpoint OOF stack: augments landmark RSF with oof_death_risk feature
ensemble_method: 30/70 rank blend with augmented landmark replacing original
notes: CV-LB inversion #2: +0.0094 CV on landmark, −0.025 LB overall. Cross-endpoint stack didn't transfer.
